message:
test(gui/overlay): pin dirty-rect merge invariants — adjacency, separation, viewport clipping (ft-1memj.28/B, ft-gznfi)

The pre-existing test_dirty_rect_merge covered exactly one case: two
abutting ranges merging into one. That's enough to catch a totally
broken merge but not the invariants the renderer actually relies on
when deciding what to repaint.

Add seven targeted unit tests around merge_dirty_results in
quickselect.rs:

* dirty_rect_non_adjacent_ranges_stay_separate
    Ranges with a gap between them MUST remain two distinct rects;
    collapsing them into a hull would cause needless repaints.
* dirty_rect_overlapping_ranges_collapse_to_one
    Overlapping dirty ranges across delegate + overlay collapse into
    a single contiguous rect (RangeSet semantics).
* dirty_rect_clips_to_visible_viewport
    Dirty rows entirely outside the visible window are dropped, and
    rows that straddle the edge are clipped to the visible portion.
    Prevents the renderer from invalidating off-screen geometry.
* dirty_rect_empty_overlay_leaves_delegate_clipped
    Common case (overlay dormant) — the result is the delegate, clipped.
* dirty_rect_both_empty_produces_empty_set
    No-op stays a no-op.
* dirty_rect_back_to_back_single_rows_merge_into_one
    Three abutting single-row entries collapse into [5..8].
* dirty_rect_out_of_viewport_ranges_drop_completely
    Every dirty range fully outside the viewport drops; merged set
    is empty.

No production change. Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo test -p frankenterm-gui dirty_rect

8 dirty_rect tests pass (the original + 7 new). Closes the second
unit-test row in ft-1memj.28's GPU-unrequired list.
